title: Chicken Nuggets

overview.description: Tender pieces of chicken coated in crisp breadcrumbs and baked or fried until golden.

method: Cut the chicken thighs into bite-sized nugget pieces.
Set up three shallow bowls: one with the flour seasoned with salt and pepper, one with the beaten eggs, and one with the breadcrumbs mixed with the paprika, garlic powder and onion powder.
Coat each piece of chicken in the seasoned flour.
Coat thoroughly in the seasoned breadcrumbs.
Place the coated nuggets on a tray.
For baking, preheat the oven to 220°C (200°C fan).
Lightly brush or spray the nuggets with oil.
Bake for 18–22 minutes, turning halfway through, until golden and the chicken is cooked through.
Alternatively, shallow-fry or deep-fry until golden and cooked through.
Allow to cool slightly before serving.

learning.did-you-know: Chicken nuggets became popular as a convenient food in the second half of the twentieth century, but the basic idea of breaded and fried pieces of meat is much older.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How to create a simple breadcrumb coating and prepare a homemade freezer-friendly alternative to commercially produced nuggets.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: A genuinely useful homemade freezer snack that is popular with children and gives you control over the ingredients without making the recipe complicated.
chapman-tips: Chicken thigh stays juicier than breast, making it particularly forgiving for nuggets.
Keep one hand for the dry ingredients and one hand for the egg to avoid coating your fingers.
Don't overcrowd the baking tray or the coating will steam rather than crisp.
A wire rack set over a baking tray helps hot air circulate around the nuggets.
For a finer, more even coating, use panko breadcrumbs or pulse regular breadcrumbs briefly in a food processor.
notes.batch-cooking: Freeze the breaded, uncooked nuggets in a single layer until solid, then transfer to a freezer bag or container. Cook directly from frozen, adding approximately 5–10 minutes to the cooking time.
notes.leftovers: Cooked nuggets can be refrigerated and reheated in an oven or air fryer. They are also useful chopped into wraps or salads.
